symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden fast, irregular heartbeat (palpitations, 'hammering out of rhythm')

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New rapid irregular tachyarrhythmia is a cardinal thyrotoxic cardiac manifestation and a storm criterion.
Irregularly irregular racing heartbeat is the patient-reportable proxy for AF.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe heat intolerance with drenching sweats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Heat intolerance and sweating reflect the hypermetabolic thyrotoxic state.
Sustained heat intolerance requiring maximal cooling is not a feature of a discrete panic attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss ~8 kg over 3 months with preserved appetite

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weight loss despite normal intake is a classic hypermetabolic thyrotoxic feature and dates the chronic background.
Objective sustained weight loss is not explained by anxiety.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe agitation, restlessness and altered mental state

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuropsychiatric disturbance (agitation/delirium) is a defining thyroid-storm criterion.
Hyperpyrexia is a key thyroid-storm criterion; the patient-reported fever is the telehealth proxy for the measured 38.1 C.
Fever also raises sepsis, which must be excluded in hospital and can precipitate storm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound proximal muscle weakness (cannot climb stairs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thyrotoxic proximal myopathy is a recognised chronic manifestation.
Fine tremor is a classic thyrotoxic sign — a clinician examination finding, not remotely measurable (a visibly trembling hand may be video-observable but the fine tremor itself is an exam sign).
Brisk reflexes support a thyrotoxic hyperadrenergic state; a clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable irregular nodular goitre with multiple firm nodules

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A nodular (rather than diffuse) goitre points to toxic multinodular goitre over Graves; requires palpation, sealed as a clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of exophthalmos and pretibial myxoedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent extrathyroidal Graves signs favour toxic multinodular goitre; a clinician examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irregularly irregular pulse / atrial fibrillation on cardiac examination and ECG

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An irregularly irregular rhythm confirmed on auscultation/ECG establishes AF — a clinician/equipment finding the AI Doctor infers from the reported irregular palpitations, not a measurement.
Measured fever is a Burch-Wartofsky storm criterion; the clinician measurement is sealed, the AI relies on the reported subjective fever.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thyroid function tests (suppressed TSH with elevated free T4/T3)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3)
investigation_details.expected_result: Suppressed TSH with elevated free T4 and/or free T3 confirming thyrotoxicosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Thyrotoxicosis unconfirmed biochemically; storm severity and treatment response cannot be tracked.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biochemical confirmation of thyrotoxicosis — a hospital blood test, never available in real time in telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Patchy, nodular uptake consistent with toxic multinodular goitre rather than diffuse Graves uptake
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Underlying thyrotoxic aetiology (toxic MNG vs Graves) not distinguished — a non-urgent step done after stabilisation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patchy nodular uptake distinguishes toxic MNG from Graves; a specialist imaging investigation, not a telehealth capability and not needed before escalation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tachyarrhythmia plus fever plus CNS agitation/altered mental state on a thyrotoxic background is the defining triad of impending thyroid storm and demands emergency escalation.
Heat intolerance, weight loss with preserved appetite and proximal myopathy establish the three-month thyrotoxic background that decompensated into crisis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer with continuous cardiac monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive 'investigation and treatment' pathway is hospital-based; continuous cardiac monitoring is required for the rapid AF and impending storm.
Confirms atrial fibrillation and assesses rate/ischaemia — a hospital/paramedic step, not a telehealth capability.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4, free T3)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Biochemically confirms thyrotoxicosis and gauges severity; performed in hospital, not before escalation.
Distinguishes toxic multinodular goitre from Graves; deferred until after acute stabilisation and never a telehealth step.
